Chetty (2012) bounds/frictions and labor supply elasticities
Eissa and Liebman (1996) labor supply responses to the EITC
runs.reasoningSummary: My memory-based prior is that primary earners’ intensive-margin substitution elasticities in the U.S. are usually modest and lower than many older macro calibrations, with higher responsiveness for secondary earners than primary earners. For decile 7 primary earners in a current-law microsimulation, I therefore center on a small positive elasticity around 0.15, allowing a fairly wide upper tail but keeping most mass below about 0.3.
